studyName: Prevention of Viral Hepatitis and HIV in Drug Users - A Hepatitis B Model for HIV and an HB Vaccine Model for HIV Vaccine Trials in Drug Users (DASH)

tables.description: The subject phenotype file includes subject ID, study number, biological sex, self-report gender, race, family race, age, affection status, year screened, living in a shelter or on the street, used any drugs in the last 7 days, times used any drugs in the last 7 days, used drugs (n=7 variables: Alcohol, Marijuana, Methamphetamines, Cocaine, Cocaine, Uppers, or Downers), consider yourself (n= 3 variables: gay, straight or bisexual), times traded sex for money in the last 30 days, with how many men or women have you traded sex for money in the last 30 days, times traded sex for drugs in the last 30 days, with how many men or women have you traded sex for drugs in the last 30 days, ever received a transfusion with blood or blood products (including clotting factors), the year of your first transfusion, ever been employed as a medical, dental, public safety or other health care worker involved with human blood contact, job description, other job description, degree of blood contact, ever been vaccinated against Hepatitis B, willing to be vaccinated against hepatitis B, ever been in prison or jail for more than 24 hours, ever been tested for a sexually transmitted disease, sexually transmitted disease been tested for (n=6 variables: Syphilis, Gonorrhoea, Chlamydia, Genital herpes, Genital warts, or other), ever been diagnosed with or treated for a sexually transmitted disease (n=6 variables: Syphilis, Gonorrhoea, Chlamydia, Genital herpes, Genital warts, or other), other sexually transmitted disease, if not treated, what was the reason, ever been tested for HIV, HIV status, and willing to be vaccinated against HIV.

variables.name: Affected_status
variables.description: Which drugs have you used?: Cocaine (same as typdrgd)

variables.name: Family_race
variables.description: Family race derived from non-missing family member race (self-report)

variables.description: What was your degree of blood contact?

variables.description: If yes, what was the year of your first transfusion?

variables.description: Of these people you had sex with, what percentage of these did you use a condom with?

variables.description: Have you used any drugs in the last 7 days?

variables.description: Gender identity of participant (self-report)

variables.description: Do you consider yourself: gay, straight or bisexual?

variables.name: inj
variables.description: Have you injected drugs?

variables.description: Have you injected drugs in the last 7 days?

variables.name: jail
variables.description: Have you ever been in prison or jail for more than 24 hours?

variables.description: If yes, what was your job description?

variables.name: livst
variables.description: Are you currently living on the street?

variables.name: longinj
variables.description: How long have you injected drugs? (years)

variables.name: numtm
variables.description: How many times have you used any drugs in the last 7 days?

variables.name: other
variables.description: List other job description

variables.name: othstd
variables.description: List other sexually transmitted disease

variables.name: race
variables.description: What is your race/ethnicity? (self-report)

variables.name: reason
variables.description: If you were not treated, what was the reason?

variables.name: sexm
variables.description: How many different men have you had sex with in the past 4 weeks?

variables.description: How many different women have you had sex with in the past 4 weeks?

variables.description: Have you ever shared needles/works while injecting any drug?

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample ID, body site where sample was collected, analyte type, tumor status, and name of the center which conducted genotyping.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte Type

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: GENOTYPING_CENTER
variables.description: Name of the center which conducted genotyping

variables.name: IS_TUMOR
variables.description: Tumor status

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: De-identified Sample ID

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID
